name: glossary
context: Dojo glossary - the terms that matter when reading or authoring a node
info: Short definitions for the core Dojo vocabulary. Use this when a manifest review, spec section, or search result uses Dojo-specific terms and you want the fast version first.
triggers: what does sub mean in dojo
body:
# Dojo Glossary

This glossary is the fast path when a manifest review or search result uses Dojo-specific language.

Read [[dojo/spec#manifest-core]] for the formal rules and [[dojo/knowledge#links-backlinks-related]] for the knowledge-layer behavior.

sections.title: Ecosystem
sections.body: A root node with `parent: null`. It names the top of a skill tree such as `dojo`, `openai`, or `postgres`.

sections.title: Standard
sections.body: A protocol or contract that other skills implement or follow. In this tree, [[dojo/api]] is a standard because it defines the registry HTTP contract.

sections.title: Skill
sections.body: An executable capability that can carry scripts and schemas. Skills answer the question 'what can I do next?'

sections.title: Context node
sections.body: A knowledge-only node. It exists to teach, explain, compare, or define terms. Most mature ecosystems should have more context nodes than executable ones.

sections.title: Sub
sections.body: A specialized variant of a skill. A `sub` must have a `skill` parent, such as [[dojo/validate/schema]].

sections.title: Alias
sections.body: An alternate phrase a user or agent might search for. Aliases improve discovery when the exact `uri` or tags are not the words people naturally use.

sections.title: Backlink
sections.body: An inbound graph edge. If node A links to node B, then B has a backlink from A. Backlinks are computed automatically from links, related edges, dependencies, and wiki-links.

sections.title: Entry script
sections.body: A script that lives in a separate file and is referenced from the manifest via `scripts[].entry`. Use this mode when the logic is large enough to deserve tests or a dedicated package layout.

sections.title: Inline script
sections.body: A script embedded directly into the manifest via `scripts[].inline`. Inline mode is best for short examples that are still easy to read without leaving the manifest.

sections.title: Reading path
sections.body: The ordered sequence returned by `/v1/learn/*`. It starts with the node's sections and may then point to linked executable skills as the next actions.

links.context: How these terms behave in the knowledge layer
API surface that exposes alias, learn, and graph behavior
Checks for type and parent correctness

related.note: These terms show up constantly during authoring and review

author: dojo-community
